preview_text:
The model favors Saudi Arabia at 40%, with Cape Verde positioned as the underdog at 29%. That 11-percentage-point gap reflects a moderate preference for Saudi Arabia, though the model assigns the outsider a genuine winning chance rather than treating the match as decisive.

The draw carries 31% probability in the model's forecast, making it nearly as likely as a Cape Verde victory. The win-loss spread across all three outcomes is relatively balanced; no single result dominates the distribution heavily. This suggests a competitive encounter where multiple plausible scenarios exist, rather than one side's superiority being overwhelming.

The market consensus diverges notably from the model. Sportsbooks rate Cape Verde significantly higher at 37%, a gap of 7 percentage points above the model's estimate, while pricing Saudi Arabia 5 points lower. Polymarket prices align more closely with the sportsbook view. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence warrants noting as a material point of disagreement on which side holds true edge.
